principles: Ride easy days TRULY easy — HR ≤138, no exceptions.
Pace by HR at altitude, by power on the flats (power reads ~25–30% low above 10k ft).
Polarized: ~0 programmed tempo. Days are truly easy or true quality.
Gut-train every ride >60 min (ramp 50→70 g carb/hr by mid-June).
Fuel the work, cut the rest — deficit on easy/rest days only.
Recovery is programmed. Sleep is the #1 KPI.
Bike quality wins every conflict; strength only after easy rides.

weeks.label: Week of Jun 8 — PC race-specific climbing + Memphis engine begins
weeks.phase: Phase 0→1: Travel bridge (PC climbing intro Jun 8–9) → Engine build begins (Jun 10)
weeks.focus: Two PC altitude climbing days (pace by HR, NOT watts — let watts fall) to build race-specificity, then fly home and open the Memphis engine block. Power meter goes live this week (PC Epic Jun 8, then Memphis Jun 10) — calibrate Z2 power-to-HR. Carb gut-training ramp reaches 70 g/hr by the Jun 13 long ride. Polarized: only ONE true quality day (Jun 11). Strength only after easy rides, never within 24 h before quality. Cut weight off-bike only — fuel the hard/long days fully.

days.headline: PC big climbing day — pace by HR, not watts

sessions.title: 3:00–3:30 PC climb · ~4,000 ft
sessions.targets: Climbs HR ≤145 (ceiling 150) · let watts fall (~150–170W up high) · PACE BY HR/RPE — NOT watts
detail: Big race-specific climbing day — the whole point is time-on-climb at altitude, not power numbers. Power reads ~25–30% low up high; the watt number is a liar. Cap climbs at HR 145.
Power meter is LIVE on the Epic today — note watts for reference only, do NOT chase them. Sit and spin steep pitches; gear down rather than let HR spike.
GEARING TEST: watch whether the 34T spins out on steep pitches. If you're grinding / HR creeping >148 on the steeps, that's the case for the 32T swap before Leadville. Log it.
Pedal descents/flats with purpose (don't coast) but keep HR ≤145 — durability over heroics.
Altitude is dry — thirst lies. Hydrate ahead of thirst.

Structure: 20 min Z2 warmup (HR ≤138) → 3×10 min @ 210–225W, HR ≤155, 90 RPM, with 5 min easy between → cooldown to ~1:30 total.

This is the season's first power-based interval session — the goal is to CONFIRM power-to-HR coupling before pushing watts. If HR runs away above 155 at 210–225W, hold the lower end; do not force watts.
Sweet-spot / sub-threshold, not max effort — this raises OBLA without digging a hole. Build sustainable, not heroic.

weeks.label: Week of Jul 13 — P2P RECON (legs 1–2) → real recovery
weeks.focus: Monday IS the event: P2P Recon — legs 1–2 of the Point 2 Point course, ridden as a full dress rehearsal for Sep 5. Two objectives, in order: (1) NUTRITION — the exact race protocol, 70 g carb/hr from min 15, 500–600 mg sodium/hr, bottles pre-counted; (2) PACING — first-half discipline, HR ≤145 between climbs, climbs 140–148 (ceiling 150), pace by HR not watts. Then recover for real: it wasn't a 50-mile race, but a 4–5 h big-vert day at altitude off a taper is still a real dent — Tue–Wed rest, two easy 60–75 min Z2 rides Thu/Sat, one light 30%-load lift Fri. NO quality all week. KPI gate unchanged and overrides everything: no programmed quality until morning RHR is back under 66 AND sleep is ≥7 h.

days.headline: P2P RECON — legs 1+2. Ride it like Sep 5: fuel from min 15, first half like a recon.

PACING (objective #2): the sub-10 P2P is won in the first half. HR ≤145 between climbs no matter how good the legs feel; climbs 140–148, HARD CEILING 150. The 2025 race was lost to a first-half over-pace — today is the antidote rehearsal.

days.headline: P2P SUB-10 — ride the first half like a recon, the second half like a race

sessions.title: RACE — Park City Point 2 Point (sub-10 execution)
sessions.targets: FIRST HALF: climbs HR ≤145, sit LOW-140s for ~3 h — must feel TOO EASY through hour 4. Watts are a ceiling not a target (cap NP ~160–175W on early climbs; altitude-suppressed). NEGATIVE-SPLIT the climbing. Midpoint checkpoint: elapsed ≥50% of target. Caffeine final third ONLY.
detail: RACE MANTRA: 'Too easy through hour 4. Eat from minute 15. The race starts at hour 7.' This is the antidote to 2025 (first half HR 161 → bonk hr 7, −24% decoupling, 12:03).
PRE (2–4 h before): 270 g carb breakfast (white rice/oats + banana + honey + sports drink, low fiber/fat). Easy 10–15 min spin to warm up; do NOT do hard openers.

FIRST THIRD (start → ~mi 26): everyone sprints off the line — LET THEM. Settle to HR low-140s within 5 min; HARD cap climbs at 145. Sit-and-spin the steep pitches, gear DOWN rather than spike watts. You should feel like you haven't started racing. Correct.

MIDDLE THIRD (~mi 26–52): HR ≤145 on climbs; PEDAL the descents and flats steadily (don't coast, don't surge) and EAT there — this is where time is gained vs the field, not by climbing harder. Watts a ceiling (~160–175W on climbs, altitude-suppressed).
MIDPOINT CHECKPOINT (~mi 39): elapsed should be ≥50% of target time. If you're AHEAD of half-time, you went out too hard — ease off NOW. This single check prevents the 2025 blow-up.

LAST THIRD (~mi 52–78): NOW race. If the legs are there (and after fueling all day they should be), let climbing HR drift to 150–155 and negative-split — the finishing climbs should be your STRONGEST (2025 fell −31% here; flip that). Caffeine kicks in for these climbs back toward the finish. Empty the tank on the final descent.
Never go below half-tank on fuel — a bonk costs 30+ minutes. Eat earlier and more than feels necessary (altitude blunts gastric emptying — exactly why you gut-trained all spring).
Math: sub-10 is ~10–11% faster than 12:03 — bought by NOT bonking at hour 7 and NOT losing 31% of climbing power, NOT by new watts. A-goal sub-10; ~10:30 floor is still a massive win.

weeks.focus: Bring freshness without losing fitness. Cut volume to ~60% (taper week) then ~40% (race week), keep ONE quality touch early then only short openers, and lock in pacing + fueling. Sleep is the #1 taper KPI — RHR should fall to the low 60s. Strength tapers to 40% then STOPS. Carbo-load the final 48 h. Do NOT diet in race week. Race goal: SUB-10 (from 12:03) — won by pacing + fueling, not watts: first-half climbs HR ≤145 (sit low-140s, "too easy through hour 4"), fuel 80 g/hr from minute 15, negative-split the climbing, caffeine final third only, midpoint checkpoint ≥50% of target.

days.headline: Easy Z2 + last meaningful strength @ 40%

sessions.title: 1:00 easy Z2
sessions.targets: HR ≤138 · 130–180W feel · 85–95 RPM · no coasting
detail: Taper week opens here — volume is ~60% of build. Keep it genuinely easy; the work is already done, do NOT chase watts.
Early AM to beat the heat.
Smooth, relaxed legs — set up a quality Tue.

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips — feed even on short rides this week to keep gut trained
~30 g carb (1 gel or half-bottle mix)
Sips to finish ~20–24 oz fluid/hr, 600–1000 mg sodium/hr (Memphis heat)

sessions.title: Strength @ 40% — last meaningful lift, then stop
sessions.targets: Load cut to ~40% — maintenance only, RPE ≤6. This is the LAST real lift before the race; none in race week.
detail: After the easy ride only (never within 24 h before Tue quality — but Mon easy → Tue quality is fine since this is the easy day).
Warmup: leg swings, walking knee-hugs, BW squats ×15, walking lunges ×10/leg, glute bridges ×15.
Hex-bar deadlift 2×8 @ 40% (crisp, no grind)
Box step-ups 2×8/leg light — your most race-specific lift, keep the pattern
Walking-lunge 2×20 steps, bodyweight or very light
RKC plank 2×20–30 s + side plank 2×15 s/side
Stop here — no lifting the rest of the block.

nutrition.notes: Easy/short day — modest deficit from fat & evening carbs (the −10 lb is nearly banked by now; do NOT cut aggressively into the taper). Hold protein 155–160 g in 4–5 feedings, one within 30–45 min post-lift.
sample_meals: Breakfast: 3-egg omelet + oats w/ berries + coffee
Dinner: salmon, sweet potato, big salad (lighter on evening carbs)

days.headline: Quality — 3×6 min @ 230–240W (the one sharpening session)

sessions.title: 1:15 incl. 3×6 min @ 230–240W
sessions.targets: Reps @ 230–240W, HR 155–160, 90 RPM; 4–5 min easy between. Sharpen, do NOT bury yourself.
detail: The ONLY quality of the taper week — keep it crisp, not exhausting. If a rep feels grindy, take the watts to the low end (230W).
20 min Z2 WU (HR ≤138) including 2–3 short build-ups.
3×6 min @ 230–240W (HR 155–160), 4–5 min easy spin recovery between.
10–15 min Z2 cooldown.
This is taper sharpening: enough to keep the top end awake, short enough to leave you fresh.

fueling.do: Start carb mix during WU — feed from min 15 (keep the race habit grooved)
~30 g carb + electrolytes (between reps)
~25 g carb; finish 20–24 oz fluid/hr, 600–1000 mg sodium/hr in heat

nutrition.notes: Quality day — fuel it fully, no deficit. Carbs around the session to support the only hard effort of the week.
sample_meals: Post-ride: rice bowl w/ chicken + 30 g whey

days.headline: Easy Z2 recovery

sessions.title: 1:15 easy Z2
sessions.targets: HR ≤135 · 130–170W · 85–95 RPM · genuinely easy
detail: One of the two easy 60–75 min spins this week. Recover from Tue's quality.
No strength — lifting is done for the block.
Talk-test the whole ride; do NOT let it drift to tempo.

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips
~30 g carb
~25 g carb; 20–24 oz fluid/hr, 600–1000 mg sodium/hr in heat

nutrition.notes: Easy day — modest deficit (trim evening carbs/fat). Protein held 155–160 g across 4–5 feedings.

days.headline: Easy Z2 (second easy spin)

sessions.title: 1:00 easy Z2
detail: Second of the two easy 60–75 min spins. Legs should start to feel springy as fatigue sheds.
Keep it short and light — resist the taper urge to 'test the legs'.

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips
~30 g carb
Finish fluid 20–24 oz/hr, 600–1000 mg sodium/hr in heat

nutrition.notes: Easy day — modest deficit. Hold protein; trim fat and evening carbs.
sample_meals: Lunch: big salad w/ chicken + olive oil
Dinner: lean protein + veg + small rice

days.headline: Rest or 30-min spin

sessions.title: Rest (or ≤30 min very easy spin)
sessions.targets: Full day off, or a 30 min HR ≤130 leg-loosener if restless
detail: Sleep is the assignment — taper KPI #1. Aim for a 7.5 h opportunity.
If you spin: 30 min flat, HR ≤130, no fueling needed (short ride).
Set up Sat's longer Z2.

nutrition.notes: Rest/deficit day — trim fat & evening carbs, hold protein. Do NOT over-restrict; the taper needs glycogen.
sample_meals: Dinner: lean protein + veg, light carbs

days.headline: 2.5–3 h pure Z2 (last longer ride)

sessions.title: 2.5–3:00 pure Z2
sessions.targets: HR ≤138 · 150–180W feel · 85–90 RPM · no coasting · ZERO intensity
detail: The last longer ride before race week — pure aerobic, no surges, no tempo creep.
Keeps the engine warm without adding fatigue. Decoupling should be <~6% — confirm freshness.
Final dress-rehearsal of race fueling cadence — feed by the clock as you will on race day.

fueling.do: Start carb mix — feed from minute 15 (the race habit)
~30 g carb + electrolytes
~35 g carb (real food OK — banana/rice cake)
~35 g carb; aim ~70 g carb/hr
~35 g carb + electrolytes
~35 g carb; maintain 20–24 oz fluid/hr, 600–1000 mg sodium/hr (Memphis heat)

nutrition.notes: Longer day — fuel fully, no deficit. Front-load carbs around the ride; this is not a cut day.
sample_meals: Post: big rice + chicken bowl + whey

days.headline: Rest — close the taper week

sessions.title: Rest day
sessions.targets: Full day off. Sleep, hydrate, eat to rest-day target.
detail: Taper week #1 closes here at ~60% volume. Race week begins tomorrow at ~40%.
No bike, no gym. Protect sleep — RHR should be trending into the low 60s.

nutrition.notes: Rest/deficit day. Hold protein, trim fat & evening carbs. Last modest-deficit day before carbo-load begins later in race week.

days.headline: Short opener — 4×90 s @ 230W

sessions.title: 0:50 incl. 4×90 s @ 230W
sessions.targets: 4×90 s @ 230W, HR settling 150–158, 90 RPM; 2–3 min easy between. Keep the legs sharp, NOT tired.
detail: Race week begins — ~40% volume, short daily openers only.
20 min Z2 WU, HR ≤138.
4×90 s @ 230W, full easy recovery between (2–3 min). These wake the legs without cost.
10 min easy cooldown.
No strength this week and the rest of the block — lifting is done.

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips — keep the gut trained right into race week
~25 g carb; finish 20–24 oz fluid/hr, 600–1000 mg sodium/hr in heat

nutrition.notes: Easy/short day. Stop dieting now — race week is NOT a cut. Eat to maintenance, hold protein, keep carbs steady to top off glycogen.

days.headline: Short opener + travel; front-load altitude hydration

sessions.title: 0:45 incl. 3×90 s @ 230W (or travel-day spin)
sessions.targets: 3×90 s @ 230W, HR 150–158, full recovery; otherwise short flat spin if traveling early
detail: Short opener before/around travel to PC. If the travel day is tight, ride 30–40 min very easy with a couple of 90 s pickups.
Begin aggressive hydration for altitude (altitude diuresis) — no alcohol from here to the race.
Arrive PC; ease into the elevation.

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips
~25 g carb; finish fluid — then keep sipping all travel day for altitude

sessions.title: Travel Memphis → Park City
sessions.targets: Hydrate hard, move/stretch in transit, protect sleep on arrival
detail: Front-load fluids + electrolytes (altitude is dry and diuretic).
No alcohol. Walk/stretch during layovers.
Bed early on arrival — sleep is the #1 taper KPI.

nutrition.notes: Easy/travel day — eat to maintenance, NOT a deficit. Extra fluids + sodium for altitude. Steady carbs to keep topping off glycogen ahead of the load.
sample_meals: Travel: rice bowl / sandwich + electrolyte drink

days.headline: Altitude acclimation spin (HR-paced)

sessions.title: 0:45–1:00 easy at altitude
sessions.targets: HR ≤138, PACE BY HR not watts (power reads ~25–30% low up high) · let watts fall · no climbing tests
detail: First full day at PC altitude — easy spin only. Do NOT test a climb.
Pace strictly by HR/RPE; the watt number lies up high.
Loosen the legs, let the body start adapting. Hydrate hard.
Pre-ride the start area / logistics if convenient.

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips — altitude blunts gastric emptying, so practice eating early
~25 g carb; fluid 16–20 oz/hr, 500–600 mg sodium/hr (altitude, dry)

nutrition.notes: Easy day at altitude — eat to maintenance. Keep carbs steady (glycogen + altitude adaptation). Hydrate aggressively with electrolytes.

days.headline: Short opener + START 48 h carbo-load (≥200 g)

sessions.title: 0:40 incl. 3×90 s building to HR 145
sessions.targets: 3×90 s building to HR 145 on a gentle grade (PACE BY HR), full recovery; everything else easy ≤138
detail: Short terrain opener at altitude — calibrate effort on a real grade, HR-capped at 145.
Let watts fall; the climb feel matters, not the number.
Keep total time short — freshness is the job now.
Lay out and pre-count race nutrition tonight (gels/bottles to 80 g carb/hr).

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips; fluid 16–20 oz/hr, 500–600 mg sodium/hr (altitude)
~20 g carb to finish (short ride)

nutrition.carb_g: ≥200 g COMPLEX carb (CARBO-LOAD begins) — push toward 500+ total
nutrition.notes: 48-h carbo-load starts today: deliberately ≥200 g complex carb on top of normal eating, building glycogen stores. NOT a deficit day — eat up. Keep fiber moderate. Hold protein.
sample_meals: Breakfast: big oats + banana + honey + eggs
Lunch: rice bowl w/ chicken + extra rice
Dinner: pasta w/ lean protein + bread

days.headline: Tiny opener + 24 h load (700 g, low fiber/fat); the sleep that matters

sessions.title: 0:30 easy + 3×60 s @ 230W
sessions.targets: 3×60 s @ 230W feel (HR-paced ~145–150 at altitude), full recovery; rest very easy
detail: Last spin before the race — tiny, just to keep the legs awake. Do NOT add fatigue.
Pace by HR; watts irrelevant at altitude.
Pre-count EVERY bottle/gel to 80 g carb/hr; lay out the cold-start kit.
This is the night that matters for race sleep — eat dinner early, screens off 45 min before bed, bedtime early.

fueling.do: Sips + ~20 g carb (very short ride — keep it minimal)

nutrition.fat_g: ~50 (kept low to fit the carb volume + ease digestion)
nutrition.notes: 24-h carbo-load: ~700 g carb, LOW fiber and LOW fat to top off glycogen and keep the gut settled for race morning. Easily-digested carbs all day; stop heavy eating early evening. Hydrate + electrolytes. This is a fueling day, not a training day.
sample_meals: Lunch: white rice + skinless chicken (low fat/fiber)
Afternoon: pretzels, gummies, sports drink, ripe banana
Dinner (early): white pasta + marinara + small lean protein

days.headline: P2P SUB-10 — ride the first half like a recon, the second half like a race

sessions.title: RACE — Park City Point 2 Point (sub-10 execution)
sessions.targets: FIRST HALF: climbs HR ≤145, sit LOW-140s for ~3 h — must feel TOO EASY through hour 4. Watts are a ceiling not a target (cap NP ~160–175W on early climbs; altitude-suppressed). NEGATIVE-SPLIT the climbing. Midpoint checkpoint: elapsed ≥50% of target. Caffeine final third ONLY.
detail: RACE MANTRA: 'Too easy through hour 4. Eat from minute 15. The race starts at hour 7.' This is the antidote to 2025 (first half HR 161 → bonk hr 7, −24% decoupling, 12:03).
PRE (2–4 h before): 270 g carb breakfast (white rice/oats + banana + honey + sports drink, low fiber/fat). Easy 10–15 min spin to warm up; do NOT do hard openers.

FIRST THIRD (start → ~mi 26): everyone sprints off the line — LET THEM. Settle to HR low-140s within 5 min; HARD cap climbs at 145. Sit-and-spin the steep pitches, gear DOWN rather than spike watts. You should feel like you haven't started racing. Correct.

MIDDLE THIRD (~mi 26–52): HR ≤145 on climbs; PEDAL the descents and flats steadily (don't coast, don't surge) and EAT there — this is where time is gained vs the field, not by climbing harder. Watts a ceiling (~160–175W on climbs, altitude-suppressed).
MIDPOINT CHECKPOINT (~mi 39): elapsed should be ≥50% of target time. If you're AHEAD of half-time, you went out too hard — ease off NOW. This single check prevents the 2025 blow-up.

LAST THIRD (~mi 52–78): NOW race. If the legs are there (and after fueling all day they should be), let climbing HR drift to 150–155 and negative-split — the finishing climbs should be your STRONGEST (2025 fell −31% here; flip that). Caffeine kicks in for these climbs back toward the finish. Empty the tank on the final descent.
Never go below half-tank on fuel — a bonk costs 30+ minutes. Eat earlier and more than feels necessary (altitude blunts gastric emptying — exactly why you gut-trained all spring).
Math: sub-10 is ~10–11% faster than 12:03 — bought by NOT bonking at hour 7 and NOT losing 31% of climbing power, NOT by new watts. A-goal sub-10; ~10:30 floor is still a massive win.

fueling.do: Start with 2 full bottles (one carb mix, one electrolyte). 270 g carb breakfast already in 2–4 h ago.
FIRST fuel — set a timer. Begin 80 g carb/hr from minute 15 (do NOT wait): ~20 g carb (1 gel or chews) + drink
~20 g carb + sips — 500–600 mg sodium/hr, 16–24 oz fluid/hr (altitude is dry — thirst lies)
~20 g carb + electrolytes; ~80 g carb in for hour 1 — confirm you're on pace
~20 g carb + drink
~20 g carb + sips; sodium 500–600 mg/hr, stay ahead of thirst
~20 g carb + electrolytes (real food OK — banana/rice cake on a flat)
~25 g carb + drink — eat on the descents/flats, not mid-steep-climb
~25 g carb + sips
~25 g carb + electrolytes; refill bottles at aid — pre-counted to 80 g/hr
~25 g carb + drink (you should still feel too easy here — keep eating)
~25 g carb + sips; sodium check 500–600 mg/hr (1000 if unusually hot)
~25 g carb + electrolytes — MIDPOINT zone: confirm elapsed ≥50% of target; if ahead, ease off
~25 g carb + drink; refill bottles, top off sodium
~25 g carb + sips — never below half-tank
~25 g carb + electrolytes
~25 g carb + drink; FINAL THIRD approaching — take CAFFEINE now (~270–530 mg total, 3–6 mg/kg, saved for here)
~25 g carb + sips — 'the race starts at hour 7' — now let HR drift to 150–155 on climbs
~25 g carb + electrolytes; keep fueling — bonk prevention to the line
~25 g carb + drink; sodium top-off, push the finishing climbs
~20 g carb + sips — negative-split the climbing, empty the tank
Final gel + drink if stomach allows; pedal the last descent hard to the finish

nutrition.carb_g: 270 g breakfast + ~80 g/hr in-race (~720–800 g on-bike) + recovery carbs
nutrition.notes: RACE DAY — do NOT diet. 270 g carb breakfast 2–4 h pre (low fiber/fat). In-race: 80 g carb/hr from minute 15, 500–600 mg sodium/hr (1000 if hot), 16–24 oz fluid/hr. Caffeine 3–6 mg/kg in the FINAL THIRD only. Post-race: aggressive carb + protein refeed for recovery.
sample_meals: Pre (2–4 h): white rice/oats + banana + honey + sports drink = 270 g carb, low fiber/fat
In-race: gels/chews/drink mix per fueling schedule = ~80 g carb/hr
Final third: caffeinated gel/drink (~270–530 mg total caffeine)
Immediately post: recovery shake (carb + 30–40 g protein) + banana
Dinner: big mixed plate — rice/pasta + protein + veg, rehydrate w/ electrolytes
